title: cenekia rapunculoides var. racemosa
intro: Cenekia rapunculoides var. racemosa, also known as the celandine poppy or celandine, is a plant species belonging to the Papaveraceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating liver and gallbladder issues due to its hepatoprotective and choleretic effects. The health benefits of celandine include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as an antioxidant, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, celandine is often used as a tincture, decoction, or infusion, and can be applied topically to skin issues such as warts and eczema.
